title: Recruitment Activities Outside Registered Address by Licensed Agencies
content:
As a general rule, a licensed agency cannot conduct recruitment activities outside of its registered address. However, it may conduct recruitment activities under the following instances:

1. If recruitment is conducted under the Public Employment Service Office Act of 1999 (RA 8759), which institutionalizes a national facilitation service network through the establishment of a Public Employment Service Office in every province, key city, and other strategic areas throughout the country.

2. If recruitment is conducted under a Special Recruitment Authority issued by the Administration. Special Recruitment Authority refers to the authority granted to an agency to conduct recruitment outside its registered business address approved by the Administration.

metadata.source_file: 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs Primer.pdf
